steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $\frac{20{x}^{2}}{7}+5x-\frac{63}{5}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Multiply the cross terms and constant
steps.content:
$$\left(\frac{4}{7}x\right)(-7)=-4x$$
$$\left(\frac{9}{5}\right)(5x)=9x$$
The $x$-terms combine to $-4x+9x=5x$, and the constant is $$\frac{9}{5}\cdot(-7)=-\frac{63}{5}.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: So $$g(x)h(x)=\frac{20}{7}x^2+5x-\frac{63}{5}.$$ That matches the computed answer, so D is correct. The coefficient, middle term, and constant all match D, so no rearrangement of the expression is needed.
